title: A Walk in the Park with Sakura

start.text: Hey! What a coincidence running into you here! *twirls happily* I was just looking for the perfect cherry blossom to sketch.

choices.text: This park is beautiful in spring.

draw.text: Yeah! I am not very good yet, but practice makes perfect, right? *holds up sketchbook* See? This one is a sparrow I drew yesterday!

choices.text: That is really cute! You have talent.

park.text: Right?! I love how the petals float down like pink snow! *catches a petal* Every year I try to press them in a book, but they always crumble.

choices.text: Maybe you need to dry them first.
The memory is what matters most.

company.text: Really?! *beams with joy* I would love that! I was getting a bit lonely sketching by myself. Two pairs of eyes are better than one for finding the best blossoms!

talent.text: You think so? *blushes* That means a lot... Most people just say it is cute and move on. But you really mean it, don't you?

choices.text: I always mean what I say to you.

draw_me.text: Ooh, a portrait? *eyes light up* Hold still! Let me capture your good side... actually, all your sides are good! *giggles and starts sketching*

tip.text: Dry them? That is genius! Why did I never think of that? *scribbles a note* You are like a walking encyclopedia! I am writing this down!

memory.text: *smiles warmly* That is such a sweet way to think about it. You know what? I will remember this moment forever. The blossoms, the breeze, and... you.

choices.text: I will remember it too, Sakura.

end_happy.text: Today is the best day ever! *laughs and spins under the cherry blossoms* Let us make lots more memories like this!

end_best.text: *looks at you with sparkling eyes* You know... meeting you was the luckiest thing that ever happened to me in this park. *hugs sketchbook tightly* I am going to draw this moment so I never forget.
